influential_news.description: Five or six influential current reputation/news stories about the target brand, selected after broad discovery and scored reduction.

items.description: One influential reputation or news story.

date.description: Exact publication date, for example '19 November 2025'.

source_type.description: One of national_business_press, trade_press, financial_investor_press, consumer_press, review_platform, regulatory_or_legal, analyst_or_research, industry_body, owned_newsroom, social_or_forum.

sentiment.description: One of positive, neutral, negative, or mixed.

influence_score.description: Overall weighted influence score from 1 to 100.

influence_subscores.description: Component scores used to calculate influence_score.

reputation_risk_or_opportunity.description: Reputation risk or opportunity score from 1 to 100.

rank_reason.description: Short explanation of why this story ranks where it does.

url.description: Verifiable URL for the story or source page.

why_it_matters.description: Why this story matters for reputation, buyer perception, marketing, or sales.

influence_ranking.description: Documentation of the broad-first scored reduction method used to select the final influential stories.

candidate_story_count.description: Number of candidate stories considered before final reduction; must be at least 12.

candidate_pool_summary.description: At least 12 concise candidate story summaries considered before final selection.

broad_discovery_queries.description: Broad discovery queries used before choosing final stories.

verification_queries.description: Targeted verification queries used after candidate scoring.

discovery_sequence.description: Ordered summary of discovery, scoring, verification, and final reduction steps.

ranking_method.description: Explanation of the ranking method and how scores were calculated.

search_queries.description: All important search queries used in discovery and verification.

ranking_factors.description: The six ranking factors: source_authority, buyer_relevance, reputation_risk_or_opportunity, evidence_quality, novelty, recency.

score_weights.description: Weights used to calculate influence_score: 0.25, 0.25, 0.20, 0.15, 0.10, 0.05 for the six ranking factors.

reputation_risk_or_opportunity.description: Weight for reputation risk or opportunity.

confidence_score.description: Confidence score from 70 to 100 for the final reputation set.

confidence_rationale.description: Why this selection is sufficiently rigorous and trustworthy.
